data.title: SA Asks: Who will Trump end up nominating for Fed chair?
data.description:
Who will President Trump end up nominating to succeed Jerome Powell as chair of the Federal Reserve?

So far, three names are rumored to have moved to the top of the president's list: National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett, Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh, and Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller.

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts MTS Insights and Justin Purohit who they thought Trump would end up picking.

MTS Insights: It looks like the decision has been narrowed down to three main candidates: Kevin Hassett, Kevin Warsh, and Christopher Waller. In my mind, the decision comes down to choosing between someone who would be loyal to Trump's calls for low rates (Hassett) and someone who would be most preferred by markets (Waller). 

If President Trump has the most agency in this decision, I think he will choose Hassett. If this is the case, I would expect a reaction in markets that sends gold and equities higher, and rates and the USD lower. Within equities, rate-sensitive assets would likely see the strongest upswings, like the Russell 2000, tech stocks (XLK), and homebuilders (XHB).

Justin Purohit: I believe President Trump will nominate Kevin Hassett as the next Fed chair. He’s clearly the most aligned with Trump’s economic worldview and, just as importantly, the most malleable choice if the administration wants to lean harder on rate cuts. 

In my view, a more dovish, potentially less independent Fed under Hassett likely means looser policy through the second half of calendar year 2026. For market observers seeking positioning, I would favor beaten-down, rate-sensitive names like Dow Chemical (DOW), Alexandria Real Estate (ARE), and LyondellBasell (LYB) over the more stretched plays across the broader markets.

data.title: ServiceNow deal for Armis makes sense: Raymond James
data.description:
• ServiceNow's (NOW) $7.75B deal for Armis makes strategic sense, Raymond James said on Tuesday.
  • “We believe this deal could provide ServiceNow with the capabilities to extend or enhance its existing OT offerings (e.g., OT Asset Management) by acquiring a best-of-breed OT security vendor and accelerating its expansion into industrial and critical-infrastructure environments,” Raymond James analysts wrote in a note to clients. “The combination of the two companies can enable customers to better discover, manage, and secure both their IT and OT assets. ServiceNow and Armis have been deeply integrated since 2020 through the Service Graph Connector, providing ServiceNow with a 'try-before-you-buy' view into joint customer usage and the demand for deeper, native security capabilities.”
  • ServiceNow shares fell 2.2% in late trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Nasdaq is the best performing financial exchange stock YTD
data.description:
The S&P 500 financials sector has largely kept pace with the broader market in 2025, rising about +14% year to date.

Financials rank as the fourth-best performing sector in the S&P 500 (SP500).

As we look at the sub-sectors within the broader financial sector, below is a list of the top 10 Financial Exchanges and Data stocks ranked by their YTD performance. The ranking includes each company’s Quant rating.

The list is topped by Nasdaq (NDAQ), with a YTD performance of +26.06%. CME Group (CME) and Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) are next, with Moody’s (MCO) and S&P Global (SPGI) rounding out the rest of the top five. All five of these companies have posted positive YTD returns.

At the bottom end of the ranking, several stocks show negative YTD performance. Morningstar (MORN) ranks last with a YTD decline of -35.63%, while MarketAxess Holdings (MKTX) and Tradeweb Markets (TW) also posted significant losses.

Here is the list:

  1. Nasdaq (NDAQ), YTD Perf: +26.06%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.36)

  2. CME Group (CME), YTD Perf: +17.64%, Quant Rating: Hold (2.97)

  3. Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), YTD Perf: +8.35%, Quant Rating: Sell (2.46)

  4. Moody’s (MCO), YTD Perf: +7.66%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.33)

Financials ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO)

data.title: U.S. F-35 fleet fell short of readiness targets, audit finds
data.description:
U.S. F-35 fighter jets were mission-ready only about half the time last year, falling well short of required performance levels, according to a report released by the Pentagon’s inspector general.

The watchdog said the fleet’s average availability rate in 2024 was roughly 50%, about 17 percentage points below the minimum standard set by the Defense Department. The report attributed the shortfall in part to weaknesses in how the Pentagon oversaw Lockheed Martin (LMT), the program’s prime contractor, particularly in enforcing sustainment and maintenance obligations.

According to the audit, contract terms governing aircraft readiness and performance lacked clear, measurable benchmarks, limiting the government’s ability to penalize poor results. The inspector general also said oversight gaps extended to material inspections and reporting requirements for government-owned property.

Despite the performance issues, the Pentagon paid Lockheed Martin (LMT) approximately $1.7 billion without applying financial penalties or adjustments, even though the aircraft failed to meet minimum availability targets and were grounded roughly half the time, the report found.

The F-35 program is the U.S. military’s most expensive weapons system, with total lifetime costs including procurement, operations and sustainment projected to exceed $2 trillion.

data.title: Marvell in focus as Citi adds 30-day catalyst watch
data.description:
• Marvell (MRVL) was in focus on Tuesday as Citi added a 30-day catalyst watch to the semiconductor company, pointing to next month's CES technology conference.
  • Shares rose 3.4% in afternoon trading.
  • “We believe investor concerns around XPU competition at two hyperscale customers and optical/DSP opportunity at 1.6T are misguided, with post-earnings weakness in shares a buying opportunity,” analyst Atif Malik wrote in a note to clients. “We expect Marvell and peers to highlight growing adoption of scale up networking including CPO at CES, which likely helps Marvell to win Trainium 4. Our Apr-Q estimates are 2% above [the] Street.” Malik has a Buy rating and $114 price target on Marvell.

data.title: Aflac says June data breach affected 22.6M individuals
data.description:
When Aflac (AFL) disclosed in June that hackers had accessed customer data, the insurer said it wasn't able to determine the number of individuals affected by the data breach. Late last week, the company confirmed that the cyberattack affected data for ~22.65M individuals.

In a filing with the Texas Attorney General, the company said stolen data included customer names, birthdates, home addresses, government-issued ID numbers, Social Security numbers, driver's license numbers, and medical and health insurance information, TechCrunch reported on Tuesday.

"To date, Aflac is not aware of any fraudulent use of personal information and — along with third-party partners — will continue to monitor any fraudulent activity," the company said in a statement on Dec. 19.

Aflac (AFL) has started notifying the affected individuals and is detailing the resources it's making available to them.

data.title: Sable shares jump after federal approval of California pipeline
data.description:
A U.S. pipeline regulator has cleared the way for Sable Offshore Corp. (SOC) to restart a controversial oil pipeline along California’s coast, a decade after a spill released thousands of barrels of crude near Santa Barbara.

Sable said the Department of Transportation’s Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration (PHMSA) approved its restart plan for portions of the Las Flores Pipeline System. Shares of the Houston-based company jumped about 30% on Tuesday following the announcement.

The decision follows PHMSA’s determination last week that key segments of the pipeline fall under federal, rather than California, jurisdiction, a finding that is likely to be challenged by state officials and environmental groups.

Sable has been seeking to revive offshore oil production assets it acquired from Exxon Mobil (XOM) last year, but progress has been slowed by opposition in California, where memories of the 2015 Refugio spill remain fresh. That accident, tied to a pipeline then known as Line 901, fouled beaches, damaged tourism and killed wildlife.

PHMSA approved the restart of two lines, CA-324 and CA-325, which move crude oil from coastal pump stations inland. CA-324 was the line responsible for the Refugio spill when it was operated by Plains All American.

California lawmakers and environmental advocates criticized the federal action, arguing it sidesteps state oversight and increases the risk of another spill. State officials said they are reviewing PHMSA’s jurisdictional determination and weighing potential responses.

Environmental groups, including the Center for Biological Diversity, said they are prepared to pursue legal options, noting that a federal consent decree tied to the 2015 spill remains in effect and still requires state approvals.

The dispute underscores broader tensions between federal efforts to expand domestic oil production under President Donald Trump and California’s push to restrict drilling and protect coastal ecosystems.

data.title: What investors can expect from Costco in 2026
data.description:
Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) enters 2026 with solid operating momentum but a sluggish share price performance over the last year amid concerns about valuation. While the stock has continued to outperform broader retail averages over the past five years, debate persists about when the next rally might begin.

 

 

 

 

Operationally, Costco (COST) is exiting 2025 with high-single-digit top-line growth and mid-single-digit comparable sales, a pace that looks sustainable into 2026 if macro conditions hold. With its first-quarter earnings report, Costco (COST) reported total sales increased 8.2% to $66.0 billion and comparable sales rose 6.4%. At the end of the quarter, Costco (COST) operated 923 warehouses, including 633 in the United States and Puerto Rico, 114 in Canada, 42 in Mexico, 37 in Japan, 29 in the United Kingdom, 20 in Korea, and 15 in Australia. Costco (COST) is planning around 35 new warehouses in fiscal 2026, including relocations.

Membership fee income rose faster than total sales for Costco (COST) during its last fiscal year and continues to be a core earnings driver. Another bright spot is e-commerce, where Costco (COST) has outpaced Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), and Amazon (AMZN). Costco (COST) has increased its tech prowess in general, including rolling out pre-scanning of baskets before checkout and mobile Scan & Go / Scan & Pay programs where members scan items with their phones, pay in-app, and show a QR code at exit. On the AI front, Costco (COST) is using advanced AI to sharpen inventory flows, model demand trends, run personalized marketing campaigns, and improve product search effectiveness.

For investors, Costco's (COST) strong balance sheet and free cash flow profile keep the door open to another sizable special dividend in 2026, though timing is uncertain since it has only been since early 2024 when the last special dividend was announced. After a powerful multi-year run, Costco (COST) is a logical candidate for a stock split if management wants to keep the nominal share price more accessible, especially after peers like Walmart (WMT) and other retail leaders have split in the last few years.

Wall Street analysts have a consensus Buy rating on Costco (COST), while Seeking Alpha analysts are more cautious with a consensus Hold rating.

data.title: Trump vents over 'good news is bad news' dynamics
data.description:
President Donald Trump reminisced about "the old days," noting that "when there was good news, the Market went up," in a social media post on Tuesday.  "Nowadays, when there is good news, the Market goes down, because everybody thinks that Interest Rates will be immediately lifted to take care of 'potential' Inflation."

He lauded the Q3 GDP report, saying it grew 4.2%, beating the 2.5% rate that was predicted. According to the Bureau of Economic Analysis, Q3 GDP rose at an annualized rate of 4.3%. That compared with the consensus of +3.2%.

Trump connected markets dynamics with his pick for the next Fed Chair. "I want my new Fed Chairman to lower Interest Rates if the Market is doing well, not destroy the Market for no reason whatsoever," he wrote on Truth Social. "I want to have a Market the likes of which we haven’t had in many decades, a Market that goes up on good news, and down on bad news, the way it should be, and the way it was."

As for inflation, that "will take care of itself and, if it doesn’t, we can always raise Rates at the appropriate time," he said. "But the appropriate time is not to kill Rallies, which could lift our Nation by 10, 15, and even 20 GDP points in a year — and maybe even more than that!"

The GDP report, issued on Tuesday, indicated that inflation remained above the Fed's 2% target. The PCE price index rose 2.8% in Q3 vs. +2.1% in Q2, and core PCE, which excludes food and energy, climbed 2.8% vs. +2.1% in the prior quarter.

Currently, traders expect the Fed to keep rates unchanged at 3.50%-3.75% at its January 27-28 meeting, with an 86.7% probability. For the March meeting, trading indicates a 54.4% chance that the rate will remain unchanged, a 40.7% probability it will be cut by 25 basis points to 3.25%-3.50%, and a 5.0% probability the benchmark rate will drop to 3.00%-3.25%, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Here are the most and least likely M&A targets in biotech, according to Truist
data.description:
French biotech (ABVX) has emerged as the most likely acquisition target among biotechs in 2026, significantly outpacing others, while liver drug developer Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (MDGL) has become the least likely candidate, according to a survey conducted by Truist Securities.

The company has received 22 investor mentions (44% of surveyed investors) in its proprietary survey commissioned to gauge market sentiment, Truist analysts led by Gregory Renza wrote in a research note last week.

Shares of the Paris-based biotech have skyrocketed this year, with multiple reports indicating that Eli Lilly (LLY) is pursuing an M&A deal with the company whose lead asset, obefazimod, is targeted at inflammatory conditions such as ulcerative colitis.

The brokerage retained its Buy rating and $140 per share target on Abivax (ABVX), arguing that with a YTD rally of 1,600% and a positive late-stage readout for obefazimod in July, “the stock has maintained high investor interest into 2026.”

Meanwhile, Revolution Medicines (RVMD) has gotten the attention of only 8% of surveyed investors, falling far short of Abivax (ABVX), while CG Oncology (CGON) and Centessa Pharmaceuticals (CNTA) scored 6% each.

Among the least likely buyout targets, Madrigal (MDGL), which markets MASH therapy Rezdiffra, has topped the list with 14 mentions, followed by Janux Therapeutics (JANX), Solid Biosciences (SLDB), and Arcus Biosciences (RCUS), indicating investor uncertainty about their M&A prospects. 

“The data show a clear concentration of acquisition expectations around ABVX, signaling strong market speculation or perceived strategic fit,” Truist wrote.
